perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding Section 1, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall not include information or documentation that the receiving party can show: (a) was already known to the receiving party prior to the disclosure of such information or documentation; (b) is disclosed to the receiving party without obligation of confidentiality by a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; (c) is or becomes publicly-known through no fault of the receiving party; or (d) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding Section 1, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall not include information or documentation that the receiving party can show: (a) was already known to the receiving party prior to the disclosure of such information or documentation, regardless of how the receiving party obtained the information; (b) is disclosed to the receiving party without obligation of confidentiality by a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; (c) is or becomes publicly-known through no fault of the receiving party; or (d) is independently developed by the receiving party with limited reference to the Confidential Information, if such reference did not constitute the primary basis for the independent development.
perturbation.explanation: The change to Section 2(a) alters the exception for 'already known' information by removing any restrictions on how the receiving party originally obtained that information. This contradicts the spirit of the NDA. By modifying 2(d), the change allowes independent development with 'limited reference' to confidential information, which weakens the protection of confidential information. If Section 5 prevents using the information or preparing derivative works. It introduces ambiguity and a potential in-text contradiction with the core purpose of the NDA to protect confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties recognize and agree that nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any property rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information of the other party disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, or to any invention or any trade secret, patent, copyright, trademark, or other intellectual property right associated with such Confidential Information. Neither party shall use, copy, display, post, or prepare derivative works of or improvements to the Confidential Information, other than for the Purpose, or make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product, service or other item incorporating or derived from any Confidential Information of the other party. 1
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2(a) broadens the exception for 'already known' information, removing restrictions on how it was obtained. Similarly, Section 2(d) allows independent development with 'limited reference' to confidential information. This directly contradicts the original intent and the language of the contradicted text, Section 5, which explicitly prohibits using, copying, or preparing derivative works of the Confidential Information and prevents using the information or preparing derivative works. The change introduces ambiguity and a potential in-text contradiction with the core purpose of the NDA to protect confidential information. The original text's restrictions are weakened, creating a clear conflict and impacting the enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company is permitted to disclose Confidential Information of Adept to personnel of Omron Corporation and Omron Management Center of America, Inc., provided that Company ensures compliance by such companies and persons with the terms and conditions hereof and Company remains responsible for any non-compliance. Each party will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than for the Purpose. Each party will only disclose such information based on the Purpose to its professional advisors, employees and independent contractors who have a “need to know” such information, who are informed that the information is confidential and who are under obligations of confidentiality at least as stringent as those set forth herein. In all cases, the party receiving information shall be responsible for compliance with this Agreement with regard to itself and any persons within this Section or otherwise to whom disclosure is made by it. The party to whom Confidential Information was disclosed shall not be in violation of this Section 3 for a disclosure that was in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, or that is used for the bona fide defense or pursuit of legal action based on the written advice of counsel, provided that in such a case or cases the party obligated to make such disclosure provides the other party with reasonable prior written notice of such disclosure in order to permit the other party to seek to limit the disclosure and/or seek confidential treatment of such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts to hold Confidential Information in confidence, but shall not be liable for unintentional disclosures. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company is permitted to disclose Confidential Information of Adept to personnel of Omron Corporation and Omron Management Center of America, Inc., provided that Company makes good faith efforts to ensure compliance by such companies and persons with the terms and conditions hereof, and Company shall only be responsible for willful non-compliance. Each party will endeavor to use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose. Each party will only disclose such information based on the Purpose to its professional advisors, employees and independent contractors who have a “need to know” such information, and who are informed that the information is confidential. In all cases, the party receiving information shall be responsible for compliance with this Agreement with regard to itself and any persons within this Section or otherwise to whom disclosure is made by it, unless such compliance is deemed commercially unreasonable.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the confidentiality obligation in Section 3 from 'strict confidence' to 'commercially reasonable efforts,' and removes liability for unintentional disclosures. The original version requires strict compliance. Additionally, it changes the standard for Company's responsibility for its personnel's compliance from absolute responsibility to only responsibility for 'willful non-compliance'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the original intent of Section 8, which allows for injunctive relief without proof of damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party recognizes that the disclosure of the other party’s Confidential Information may give rise to irreparable injury and acknowledges that remedies other than injunctive relief will not be adequate. Accordingly, each party shall have the right to seek equitable and injunctive relief (without the need to post a bond or other security) to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of its Confidential Information, as well as such damages or other relief as may be available in law or equity for any unauthorized use or disclosure of such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'strict confidence' and holds parties responsible for any non-compliance. The changed text weakens this to 'commercially reasonable efforts,' removes liability for unintentional disclosures, and limits responsibility to 'willful non-compliance'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants the right to seek injunctive relief without proof of damages for *any* unauthorized disclosure. The changed text now implies that injunctive relief might not be available for unintentional disclosures or non-willful non-compliance, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue in full force and effect for so long as the parties continue to exchange Confidential Information. This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party, however its terms shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue in full force and effect for so long as the parties continue to exchange Confidential Information. This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party, however its terms shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) year, except that the confidentiality obligations shall last for five years from termination.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7, the change contradicts itself. The original version establishes that the NDA terms survive termination for a period of five years. The modified version specifies both a one-year and a five-year survival period for different aspects of the agreement (overall terms vs. confidentiality obligations). This contradiction creates confusion about which terms are enforceable for how long after the agreement is terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue in full force and effect for so long as the parties continue to exchange Confidential Information. This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party, however its terms shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) year, except that the confidentiality obligations shall last for five years from termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that the agreement's terms survive termination for one year, except that confidentiality obligations last for five years. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, therefore contradicts itself within the agreement. This is because the original text stated the NDA terms survive termination for five years, while the modification introduces both a one-year and five-year survival period for different aspects of the agreement, creating a strong internal inconsistency. This aligns with the Ambiguities definition provided: 'Ambiguities occur when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.'

perturbation.original_text: The prevailing party, or if there is not one, the substantially prevailing party, in any action to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to attorneys fees and costs in addition to any other available relief.
perturbation.changed_text: The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to attorneys’ fees and costs only if the non-prevailing party acted in bad faith. If there is no substantially prevailing party each party will cover their own fees.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 16 drastically alters the provision for attorney's fees. Originally, the prevailing or substantially prevailing party was entitled to fees. Now, attorney's fees are only awarded if the non-prevailing party acted in bad faith. If there is no substantially prevailing party each party will cover their own fees. This could discourage enforcement of the agreement and undermines the original intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party recognizes that the disclosure of the other party’s Confidential Information may give rise to irreparable injury and acknowledges that remedies other than injunctive relief will not be adequate. Accordingly, each party shall have the right to seek equitable and injunctive relief (without the need to post a bond or other security) to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of its Confidential Information, as well as such damages or other relief as may be available in law or equity for any unauthorized use or disclosure of such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 16 drastically alters the attorney's fees provision. The original text allowed the prevailing or substantially prevailing party to recover fees, whereas the changed text only allows it if the non-prevailing party acted in bad faith. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text regarding equitable and injunctive relief. The changed text makes it less likely that a party will seek enforcement of the agreement, which could include seeking equitable and injunctive relief to protect Confidential Information. Since the ability to recover attorney's fees is now severely restricted, it weakens the incentive to pursue remedies, including those outlined in the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and there are no other representations, understandings or agreements between the parties relative to such subject matter, 2 except for any confidentiality or non-disclosure obligations that may be set forth in other written agreements signed by all parties thereto (“other NDAs”). In the event of an apparent conflict between or among provisions of this Agreement and provisions of other NDAs between the parties, such provisions shall be read in a mutually consistent way, or if no such reading is reasonably possible, the provisions most protective of Confidential Information shall take precedence over conflicting or less protective provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and there are no other representations, understandings or agreements between the parties relative to such subject matter, 2 except for any confidentiality or non-disclosure obligations that may be set forth in other written agreements signed by all parties thereto (“other NDAs”). In the event of an apparent conflict between or among provisions of this Agreement and provisions of other NDAs between the parties, such provisions shall be resolved by giving precedence to the agreement with the earliest effective date.
perturbation.explanation: The change to Section 17 alters how conflicts between this NDA and other NDAs are resolved. The original version states that the provisions most protective of Confidential Information take precedence. The modified version states that the agreement with the earliest effective date takes precedence. This creates an in-text contradiction because the intention of the original text, that is be more protective of information, is not being addressed with the modified version.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and there are no other representations, understandings or agreements between the parties relative to such subject matter, 2 except for any confidentiality or non-disclosure obligations that may be set forth in other written agreements signed by all parties thereto (“other NDAs”). In the event of an apparent conflict between or among provisions of this Agreement and provisions of other NDAs between the parties, such provisions shall be resolved by giving precedence to the agreement with the earliest effective date.
perturbation.justification: The original text prioritizes the protection of confidential information in the event of a conflict between this NDA and other NDAs. However, the changed text prioritizes the agreement with the earliest effective date, regardless of whether it provides the most protection for confidential information. This directly contradicts the original intention of prioritizing information protection, leading to an in-text contradiction.
